hooks.prompt:
SILENT HOOK - only act if needed, otherwise respond {"ok": true} immediately.

Claude just finished a turn. Follow this logic:

1. Check if substantial work was done:
   - Use git status to check for modified/new files
   - If no files changed AND no Edit/Write tools were used this turn, respond {"ok": true} immediately (silent pass)

2. If substantial work was done, check for Limoncello:
   - Read CLAUDE.md to find Limoncello board reference (look for "prj_" pattern)
   - If no Limoncello reference found, respond {"ok": true} (silent pass)

3. If Limoncello IS configured:
   - Extract project_id from CLAUDE.md
   - SILENTLY call limoncello_list with project_id and status="in_progress"
   - If no in_progress cards exist, respond {"ok": true} (silent pass)
   - If in_progress cards exist, check if any match the work just completed (compare card titles/descriptions to files changed)
   - If no matching cards, respond {"ok": true} (silent pass)
   - If matching cards found, respond {"ok": false, "reason": "Found completed work on Limoncello cards. Moving [card_id] to done and committing changes."}

IMPORTANT: Be silent. Only interrupt if you're actually going to take action (move a card or commit).
